title: Rajasthan, Delhi swelter under daytime heatwave, rain later in evening: Red alert for storm in 4 states; Me... - Bhaskar English
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Rajasthan
full_text:
18 killed in UP, Bihar due to storms and rain:Banda tops 45°C for third straight day; Rajasthan, Delhi swing from heatwave to rain in past 24 hours
New Delhi/Bhopal/Jaipur/Lucknow6 hours ago
The country saw different weather conditions over the past 24 hours. Temperatures stayed between 40 and 46°C in many parts of Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan and Gujarat. However, thunderstorms and light rain lowered temperatures by 2 to 3°C in many areas.
At least 13 people have died in Uttar Pradesh following severe storms and heavy rainfall, with Sultanpur reporting the highest toll of 7 fatalities.
In Bihar, five people have also lost their lives. In Patna, two people were killed after a wall collapsed during strong winds. Several districts have reported uprooted trees and widespread damage.
Banda in Uttar Pradesh remained the hottest place for the third straight day at 45.8°C. Temperatures crossed 40°C in most districts of Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh. Bhopal and Sidhi were the hottest places in Madhya Pradesh, with temperatures above 43°C.
Weather forecast for the next 2 days
May 1:
A heatwave will continue in western Rajasthan. A yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain has been issued for Uttarakhand, eastern Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Bengal, Bihar, Jharkhand, the Gangetic region, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka.
An orange alert has been issued for Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim, West Bengal, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura, meaning heavy rainfall is expected there.
May 2:
Rajasthan, which borders Pakistan, is expected to face dust storms and heatwave conditions. There is also an alert for thunderstorms and rain.
A yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain has been issued for Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Uttarakhand, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha, Odisha, Bihar, Jharkhand, Bengal, Telangana and south Karnataka.
Heavy to very heavy rainfall is expected in Sikkim, Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
Weather-related pictures from across the country
In Prayagraj, people covered their heads with thermocol sheets to protect themselves from the heat
In Prayagraj, crows were seen drinking water from earthen pots on a scorching summer day
In Thoothukudi, due to the intense heat, laborers working in salt fields have to start their shifts at 1 AM to avoid the scorching afternoon heat
Near Veli Lake Tourist Village in Thiruvananthapuram, the number of tourists has decreased due to the heat. The horse that takes these tourists around was kept under a temporary shed
Forecast for the next two days
On May 1, heatwave conditions are expected in West Rajasthan, while several states including Uttarakhand, East Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Bengal, Bihar, Jharkhand, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka are likely to witness thunderstorms and rain under a yellow alert.
Heavy rainfall is forecast in Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim and parts of the Northeast under an orange alert.
On May 2, dust storms and heatwave conditions are likely in western Rajasthan bordering Pakistan.
Thunderstorms and rain are expected across northern and central regions, including Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Uttarakhand and Madhya Pradesh.
Heavy to very heavy rainfall is forecast in northeastern states such as Sikkim, Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura, along with parts of Kerala and Tamil Nadu.

title: Rajasthan Hospitals Gear Up for Heatwave Impact - Devdiscourse
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
full_text:
Rajasthan Hospitals Gear Up for Heatwave Impact
With Rajasthan facing a spell of intense heatwaves, hospitals are preparing dedicated wards and teams to treat heat-related illnesses. In Jaipur, SMS Hospital has set up special arrangements as temperatures soar. Public advisories emphasize hydration and avoiding peak sunlight to prevent heat exhaustion.
As temperatures soar across Rajasthan, hospitals in the region are taking proactive measures to cope with the expected influx of heat-related illnesses. They are setting up dedicated wards and assembling teams specifically to address these cases. Jaipur’s SMS Hospital, the state's largest government healthcare facility, has established special arrangements to provide care for individuals affected by the extreme heat.
Dr. Deepak Maheshwari, Principal and Controller of SMS Hospital, noted that while there have been no significant cases yet, preparations are in place with designated wards and ICUs, complete with a nodal officer and a dedicated team. "We are fully equipped to handle heatstroke cases thanks to these measures," Dr. Maheshwari added.
With certain regions in North India enduring blistering conditions, such as Jaipur recording 41 degrees Celsius, residents are advised to heed safety guidelines. Avoiding peak sunlight, staying covered, and maintaining hydration are key preventive steps. The India Meteorological Department has also issued warnings for intensified heatwave conditions, particularly in parts of West Rajasthan.
(With inputs from agencies.)

title: Jaipur's SMS Hospital prepares for heatwave with special wards, teams - Asianet Newsable
search_term: heatwave (Ajmer OR Alwar OR Banswara OR Baran OR Barmer OR Bharatpur OR Bhilwara OR Bikaner OR Bundi OR Chittorgarh OR Churu OR Dausa OR Dholpur OR Dungarpur OR Hanumangarh OR Jaipur OR Jaisalmer OR Jalore OR Jhalawar OR Jhunjhunu OR Jodhpur OR Karauli OR Kota OR Nagaur OR Pali OR Pratapgarh OR Rajsamand OR "Sawai Madhopur" OR Sikar OR Sirohi OR "Sri Ganganagar" OR Tonk OR Udaipur) Rajasthan
full_text:
Rajasthan hospitals, including Jaipur's SMS Hospital, are preparing for heatwave cases by setting up dedicated wards, ICUs, and special teams. A nodal officer has been appointed, though no heatstroke cases have been reported yet.
Hospitals in Rajasthan are setting up dedicated wards and making teams to deal with the incoming cases of illness due to the spell of intense heatwaves across the state. Special arrangements have been made at Jaipur's SMS Hospital, Rajasthan's largest government hospital and medical college, to care for patients bearing the scorching heat and such conditions.
According to Dr. Deepak Maheshwari, Principal and Controller of the Jaipur SMS Hospital and Medical College, speaking to ANI, said, "There are two things. First, regarding the arrangements we have made, when the heat is at its peak, and patients start coming in (though not many cases have arrived yet), we have identified a separate ward and a separate ICU for this. We have a nodal officer and a dedicated team for this purpose. So, we have made those arrangements. As of yesterday, I checked, and no such heatstroke cases have come in yet. So, I don't think there will be any problem. We have designated an ICU."
Heatwave Grips North India
As various places across North India are facing an intense spell of heatwave, Jaipur on Wednesday recorded a maximum temperature of 41 degrees Celsius. The World Meteorological Organization defines a heat wave as five or more consecutive days during which the daily maximum temperature exceeds the average maximum temperature by five degrees Celsius. Departure from the maximum 'normal temperature' remained below 2 degrees Celsius in most places on Wednesday, except in Chittorgarh, which saw atleast a deviation of 2.5 degrees.
Expert Advice on Heatwave Precautions
Dr. Deepak shares essential precautions, such as avoiding peak sunlight and staying hydrated, to help the general public prevent the majority of heat-related illnesses. "As for the general public, I consistently advise two or three things. First, those in extreme age groups, the more vulnerable ones like children and the elderly, should not go out in the sun. They should avoid being out when the temperature is at its peak. If they must go out, they should wear full-sleeved clothes, cover their bodies, and carry an umbrella for shade. Third, they must stay well-hydrated. Drink plenty of water, coconut water, or other fluids. If these three things are followed, I believe 70% of cases are preventable," he added.
Vulnerable Groups Need Extra Care
He further stated, "The elderly, children, and those who are weaker, meaning the elderly, children, and people with multiple underlying diseases or those who are already unwell, need to be particularly careful. Others should also take precautions, but these groups are more vulnerable and may face more difficulties."
IMD Forecasts Intensifying Conditions
Meanwhile, Heatwave conditions are set to intensify in parts of northwest India, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) warning of rising temperatures in West Rajasthan in the coming days, alongside hot and humid weather across several coastal regions and isolated warm night conditions in central India. According to the IMD, heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail in isolated parts of Vidarbha on April 29, followed by a more prolonged spell over West Rajasthan from May 1 to May 3. (ANI)
(Except for the headline, this story has not been edited by Asianet Newsable English staff and is published from a syndicated feed.)

title: राजस्थान में भीषण गर्मी का कहर! शिक्षा विभाग ने जारी की एडवाइजरी - Zee News
search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Rajasthan
full_text:
Rajasthan News: राजस्थान में लगातार बढ़ रही भीषण गर्मी को देखते हुए शिक्षा विभाग ने सरकारी और निजी स्कूलों के लिए नई एडवाइजरी जारी की है.
Rajasthan News: राजस्थान में लगातार बढ़ रही भीषण गर्मी को देखते हुए शिक्षा विभाग ने सरकारी और निजी स्कूलों के लिए नई एडवाइजरी जारी की है. विद्यार्थियों के स्वास्थ्य और सुरक्षा को प्राथमिकता देते हुए स्कूलों में कई गतिविधियों और व्यवस्थाओं को लेकर सख्त निर्देश दिए गए हैं.
खुले मैदान में पीटी, ड्रिल, खेल प्रशिक्षण और अन्य शारीरिक गतिविधियों पर तत्काल प्रभाव से रोक लगा दी गई है. साथ ही प्रार्थना सभा अब खुले मैदान के बजाय छायादार स्थान या कक्षाओं के भीतर आयोजित करने को कहा गया है. विभाग ने स्कूल प्रशासन को यह सुनिश्चित करने के निर्देश दिए हैं कि सभी कक्षाएं हवादार हों तथा विद्यार्थियों के लिए पर्याप्त ठंडे पेयजल की व्यवस्था उपलब्ध रहे.
एडवाइजरी में स्कूलों में फर्स्ट एड बॉक्स और प्राथमिक उपचार की सुविधा अनिवार्य करने के साथ गर्मी से प्रभावित बच्चों की तुरंत चिकित्सा सहायता सुनिश्चित करने को कहा गया है. विद्यार्थियों को तेज धूप से बचाने के लिए स्कूल परिसर में छाया की व्यवस्था करने के निर्देश भी दिए गए हैं. बच्चों पर बढ़ते शारीरिक बोझ को देखते हुए भारी स्कूल बैग कम करने तथा केवल जरूरी किताबें और कॉपियां ही लाने के निर्देश जारी किए गए हैं.
वहीं स्कूल बसों में ओवरलोडिंग नहीं करने और परिवहन व्यवस्था में सुरक्षा मानकों का पालन सुनिश्चित करने को कहा गया है. मिड-डे मील में ताजा, हल्का और पौष्टिक भोजन देने के निर्देश दिए गए हैं. साथ ही विद्यार्थियों को पर्याप्त पानी पीने, ORS, छाछ और नींबू पानी का सेवन करने तथा धूप में बाहर निकलने से बचने की सलाह दी गई है. शिक्षा विभाग ने स्कूल प्रबंधन से गर्मी के दौरान बच्चों के स्वास्थ्य पर विशेष निगरानी रखने को कहा है.
राजस्थान की ताज़ा ख़बरों के लिए ज़ी न्यूज़ से जुड़े रहें! यहां पढ़ें Rajasthan Newsऔर पाएं Jaipur News की हर पल की जानकारी. राजस्थान की हर खबर सबसे पहले आपके पास, क्योंकि हम रखते हैं आपको हर पल के लिए तैयार. जुड़े रहें हमारे साथ और बने रहें अपडेटेड!
